verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 (HSP60) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperone, but it has well-documented ecto-display on the outer leaflet of stressed and tumor cells, and on apoptotic cells, where it serves as a danger signal and is recognized by innate immunity. Surface HSP60 has been pursued as a tumor/stress-cell antigen. This fits cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation, stress, and cell-death-related surface translocation). Ruling out other buckets: not a TM/GPI protein so not classical surface; no narrow germline/developmental lineage restriction; not a lysosomal TM protein; baseline pool is mitochondrial, not a steady PM minority via cycling; no covalent TM-partner tethering documented.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and tumor-selectivity of surface HSP60 pool vs intracellular dominance; therapeutic tractability of the ecto-form remains debated.
